findings.justification: Profile links and user status strings are concatenated directly into the HTML email body without escaping, so attacker-controlled profile data could inject HTML or script content into admin notification emails.

Raw form POST data from UM()->form()->post_form is stored verbatim in user meta (um_user_blocked__metadata) without sanitization, persisting unsanitized attacker-controlled input that could cause XSS if later rendered without escaping.

A currently-logged-in suspicious user is set to 'inactive' (weaker block) rather than 'rejected', meaning an attacker who triggered detection while authenticated receives a less restrictive ban status than an unauthenticated one.
The send_notification method is public with no capability or nonce check, so any code with access to the Secure object can trigger bulk admin email notifications without authentication.

The banned capabilities list is modifiable via apply_filters, allowing any installed plugin or theme to remove critical capabilities (e.g., manage_options) from the ban list and permit registration of users with administrative privileges.
